Mock Community

Gabriela Puerres

2025-06-27

DADA2 pipeline for Mock Samples

The analysis includes:

Mock A: Zymo Fecal Reference standard (#D6323).

  1. mock-synthetic-ZYMO-1
  2. mock-synthetic-ZYMO-2
  3. mock-synthetic-ZYMO-3

Mock B: ZymoBIOMICS Microbial Community DNA Standard II (Log Distribution)

  1. mock-synthetic-ZYMO-4
  2. mock-synthetic-ZYMO-5

Our goal is to identify molecular markers and microbiome shifts associated with cow’s inflammation after parturition.

1. Denoising - DADA2

1. Load the Libraries

# Load packages 

library(dada2) 
library(phyloseq) 
library(ggplot2) 
library(ShortRead)   
library(dplyr) 
library(stats) 
library(Biostrings) 
library(readxl)

2. Set Working Directory and Paths

# Define subdirectories
path.fastq    <- file.path("Raw_Data_Mock", "Mock_Fastq")
path.meta     <- file.path("Analysis", "Meta") 
path.rds      <- file.path( "Analysis", "RDS")
path.figures  <- file.path( "Analysis", "Figures")
path.results  <- file.path( "Analysis", "Results")

# Define reference FASTA files
path.mockA.fasta <- path.mockA.fasta <- "Raw_Data_Mock/Mock_A/MockA_Reference/mockA_ASVs2_reference.fasta"
path.mockB.fasta <- "Raw_Data_Mock/Mock_B/Zymo_logDistributedDNA_ssrRNAs/zymo_species_formatted.fasta"


# Check available files
list.files(path.fastq)
##  [1] "Extracted"                                        
##  [2] "filtered"                                         
##  [3] "filtered_final"                                   
##  [4] "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-1_S85_L001_R1_paired.fastq.gz"
##  [5] "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-1_S85_L001_R2_paired.fastq.gz"
##  [6] "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-2_S86_L001_R1_paired.fastq.gz"
##  [7] "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-2_S86_L001_R2_paired.fastq.gz"
##  [8] "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-3_S87_L001_R1_paired.fastq.gz"
##  [9] "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-3_S87_L001_R2_paired.fastq.gz"
## [10] "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-4_S88_L001_R1_paired.fastq.gz"
## [11] "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-4_S88_L001_R2_paired.fastq.gz"
## [12] "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-5_S89_L001_R1_paired.fastq.gz"
## [13] "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-5_S89_L001_R2_paired.fastq.gz"
## [14] "Mock_C"

3. Filtering and Trimming

# Forward and reverse fastq filenames
fnFs <- sort(list.files(path.fastq, pattern = "R1_paired.fastq.gz", full.names = TRUE))
fnRs <- sort(list.files(path.fastq, pattern="R2_paired.fastq.gz", full.names = TRUE))

# Check if the number of forward and reverse reads matches
length(fnFs)
## [1] 5
length(fnFs) == length(fnRs)
## [1] TRUE

# Extract sample names
sample.names <- sapply(strsplit(basename(fnFs), "_"), `[`, 1:2)
sample.names <- apply(sample.names, 2, paste0, collapse = "_")
# Place filtered files in filtered/ subdirectory
filtFs <- file.path(path.fastq , "filtered", paste0(sample.names, "_F_filt.fastq.gz"))
filtRs <- file.path(path.fastq, "filtered", paste0(sample.names, "_R_filt.fastq.gz"))
names(filtFs) <- sample.names
names(filtRs) <- sample.names

# Create filtered directory if it doesn't exist
if (!dir.exists(file.path(path.fastq, "filtered_final"))) {
  dir.create(file.path(path.fastq, "filtered_final"))
}
# Filter and trim the reads
out <- filterAndTrim(fnFs, filtFs, fnRs, filtRs, 
                     trimLeft=c(20,17), 
                     truncLen=c(240,220),
                     maxN=0, 
                     maxEE=c(3,4), 
                     truncQ=2, 
                     rm.phix=TRUE,
                     compress=TRUE, 
                     multithread=TRUE)
## Multithreading has been DISABLED, as forking is not supported on .Platform$OS.type 'windows'
head(out)
##                                                   reads.in reads.out
## mock-synthetic-ZYMO-1_S85_L001_R1_paired.fastq.gz   827836    719795
## mock-synthetic-ZYMO-2_S86_L001_R1_paired.fastq.gz   892942    769245
## mock-synthetic-ZYMO-3_S87_L001_R1_paired.fastq.gz   995432    847260
## mock-synthetic-ZYMO-4_S88_L001_R1_paired.fastq.gz   880557    771752
## mock-synthetic-ZYMO-5_S89_L001_R1_paired.fastq.gz   329700    287080
# Learn error rates for forward and reverse reads
errF <- learnErrors(filtFs, randomize = TRUE, multithread = TRUE)
## 186397200 total bases in 847260 reads from 1 samples will be used for learning the error rates.
errR <- learnErrors(filtRs, randomize = TRUE, multithread = TRUE)
## 156665656 total bases in 771752 reads from 1 samples will be used for learning the error rates.
# Plot error rates
plotErrors(errF, nominalQ = TRUE)

4. Denoise the reads

# Denoise forward and reverse reads
dadaFs <- dada(filtFs, err = errF, multithread = TRUE)
## Sample 1 - 719795 reads in 31878 unique sequences.
## Sample 2 - 769245 reads in 38006 unique sequences.
## Sample 3 - 847260 reads in 42631 unique sequences.
## Sample 4 - 771752 reads in 8121 unique sequences.
## Sample 5 - 287080 reads in 4460 unique sequences.
dadaRs <- dada(filtRs, err = errR, multithread = TRUE)
## Sample 1 - 719795 reads in 24545 unique sequences.
## Sample 2 - 769245 reads in 28070 unique sequences.
## Sample 3 - 847260 reads in 32144 unique sequences.
## Sample 4 - 771752 reads in 4631 unique sequences.
## Sample 5 - 287080 reads in 2774 unique sequences.

5. Merging of Paired Reads

# Merge paired reads
mergers <- mergePairs(dadaFs, filtFs, dadaRs, filtRs, verbose=TRUE)
length(mergers) #=5 Check the number of merged reads
## [1] 5
merged_counts <-sapply(mergers, function(x) sum(x$abundance)) #How many reads were merged per sample
head(merged_counts) # table of the total merged reads 
## mock-synthetic-ZYMO-1_S85 mock-synthetic-ZYMO-2_S86 mock-synthetic-ZYMO-3_S87 
##                    668945                    714272                    781048 
## mock-synthetic-ZYMO-4_S88 mock-synthetic-ZYMO-5_S89 
##                    770697                    286698
#visualize the distribution of merged reads
hist(merged_counts, main = "Merged reads per sample",
     xlab = "Number of merged reads", ylab = "Frequency",
     col = "skyblue", breaks = 30)

6. Remove Chimeras

# Remove chimeras
seqtab <- makeSequenceTable(mergers) #Create a sequence table
dim(seqtab) #No. samples, No. sequences 
## [1]    5 3289
table(nchar(getSequences(seqtab)))
## 
##  220  221  222  223  224  225  227  228  238  243  250  254  264  265  270  274 
##  265    5  246   34    8    3    2    1    1    1    1    2    6    1    1    1 
##  306  337  339  348  360  362  363  364  365  366  367  368  369  370  371  380 
##    1    1    2    1    1    1  112 1275  195  159  276   15    3    4    7    1 
##  381  382  383  384  385  387  388  389  390  403 
##    1    4   26  346    3    1   40  201   34    1
seqtab2 <- seqtab[,nchar(colnames(seqtab)) %in% 260:400]
sum(seqtab2)/sum(seqtab) 
## [1] 0.9978139
seqtab.nochim <- removeBimeraDenovo(seqtab2, method = "consensus", multithread = TRUE, verbose = TRUE)
dim(seqtab.nochim) #No. samples, No. sequences
## [1]    5 1137
names_mock_samples <- rownames(seqtab.nochim)
names_mock_samples 
## [1] "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-1_S85" "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-2_S86"
## [3] "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-3_S87" "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-4_S88"
## [5] "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-5_S89"

7. Track Read Retention

# Track read retention through the pipeline
getN <- function(x) sum(getUniques(x))
track <- cbind(out, sapply(dadaFs, getN), 
               sapply(dadaRs, getN), sapply(mergers, getN), 
               rowSums(seqtab.nochim))

colnames(track) <- c("input", "filtered", "denoisedF", "denoisedR", "merged", "nonchim")
rownames(track) <- sample.names

track
##                            input filtered denoisedF denoisedR merged nonchim
## mock-synthetic-ZYMO-1_S85 827836   719795    718016    718917 668945  645514
## mock-synthetic-ZYMO-2_S86 892942   769245    766811    767739 714272  684529
## mock-synthetic-ZYMO-3_S87 995432   847260    844934    845841 781048  750067
## mock-synthetic-ZYMO-4_S88 880557   771752    771582    771174 770697  767489
## mock-synthetic-ZYMO-5_S89 329700   287080    287022    286972 286698  285889

8. Segregate files

# Define sample names for each mock group

mockA_samples <- c("mock-synthetic-ZYMO-1_S85", 
                   "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-2_S86", 
                   "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-3_S87") 
mockB_samples <- c("mock-synthetic-ZYMO-4_S88", 
                   "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-5_S89")

9. Generate Phyloseq Object for Positive Controls

A. Mock A: Zymo Fecal Reference standard (#D6323)

OTU Data:

#Seq
seqtab.mockA <- subset(seqtab.nochim, 
                       rownames(seqtab.nochim) %in%
                         mockA_samples) 

dim(seqtab.mockA)
## [1]    3 1137
seq.mockA <- otu_table(seqtab.mockA, 
                       taxa_are_rows = FALSE)
class(seq.mockA)
## [1] "otu_table"
## attr(,"package")
## [1] "phyloseq"
dim(seq.mockA)
## [1]    3 1137

Taxonomy Data:

# Assigment to the Genus Level
taxa.mockA <- assignTaxonomy(seqtab.mockA, 
                             refFasta = "Raw_Data_Mock/Mock_A/MockA_Reference/mockA_genus_reference.fasta") 
taxa.mockA <- phyloseq::tax_table(taxa.mockA) 
class(taxa.mockA) 
## [1] "taxonomyTable"
## attr(,"package")
## [1] "phyloseq"
dim(taxa.mockA)
## [1] 1137    6

Metadata (Sample Data):

# read metadata
meta <- read.csv(file.path(path.meta, "Final_Cow_FecalMicrobiome_Metadata.csv"))

meta_A <- meta
meta_B <- meta

row.names(meta_A) <- meta_A$Corrected_Sample_ID
mockA_samples <- c("mock-synthetic-ZYMO-1", 
                   "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-2",
                   "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-3" )
meta_mockA <- meta_A[
  row.names(meta_A) %in% mockA_samples, ]

rownames(meta_mockA) 
## [1] "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-1" "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-2" "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-3"
# The names in both, meta and seq should be the same:
sample_names(seq.mockA) <- rownames(meta_mockA)
identical(sample_names(seq.mockA), rownames(meta_mockA))#T
## [1] TRUE
meta_mockA <- sample_data(meta_mockA) #Transform the meta_mockA data in the correct component data classes
class(meta_mockA) # "sample_data" 
## [1] "sample_data"
## attr(,"package")
## [1] "phyloseq"
dim(meta_mockA)
## [1]  3 97

Generate phyloseq object for positive control:

ps.mock.A <- phyloseq(seq.mockA, taxa.mockA, meta_mockA)
ps.mock.A
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 1137 taxa and 3 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 3 samples by 97 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 1137 taxa by 6 taxonomic ranks ]
# Save phyloseq object
saveRDS(ps.mock.A, file.path(path.rds,"ps.mock.A.rds"))

B. Subgroup Mock B: ZymoBIOMICS Microbial Community DNA Standard II (Log Distribution)

OTU Data:

#Seq
seqtab.mockB <- subset(seqtab.nochim, rownames(seqtab.nochim) %in% mockB_samples) 
dim(seqtab.mockB)
## [1]    2 1137
seq.mockB <- otu_table(seqtab.mockB, taxa_are_rows = FALSE)
class(seq.mockB) # "otu_table", attr(,"package"), "phyloseq"
## [1] "otu_table"
## attr(,"package")
## [1] "phyloseq"

Taxonomy Data:

taxa.mockB <- assignSpecies(seqtab.mockB, refFasta ="Raw_Data_Mock/Mock_B/Zymo_logDistributedDNA_ssrRNAs/zymo_species_formatted.fasta")

taxa.mockB <- phyloseq::tax_table(taxa.mockB) #Convert into the right phyloseq object!
class(taxa.mockB) #"taxonomyTable" 
## [1] "taxonomyTable"
## attr(,"package")
## [1] "phyloseq"
dim(taxa.mockB)
## [1] 1137    2

Metadata (Sample Data):

# Check Metadata
dim(meta_B)
## [1] 164  97
# Select Metadata for Mock B:
mockB_samples <- c("mock-synthetic-ZYMO-4", "mock-synthetic-ZYMO-5")
meta_mockB <- meta_B[meta_B$Corrected_Sample_ID %in% mockB_samples, ]
sample_names(seq.mockB) <- rownames(meta_mockB)

identical(sample_names(seq.mockB), rownames(meta_mockB)) # True
## [1] TRUE
# Transform the meta_mockB data in the correct component data classes
meta_mockB <- sample_data(meta_mockB) 
class(meta_mockB) # "sample_data"
## [1] "sample_data"
## attr(,"package")
## [1] "phyloseq"
# Generate phyloseq object for positive control
ps.mock.B <- phyloseq(seq.mockB, taxa.mockB, meta_mockB)
ps.mock.B
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 1137 taxa and 2 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 2 samples by 97 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 1137 taxa by 2 taxonomic ranks ]
saveRDS(ps.mock.B, file.path(path.rds,"ps.mockB.rds"))

10. Merged Mock Phyloseq Object

# Merge the two phyloseq objects
ps.mock.merged <- merge_phyloseq(ps.mock.A, ps.mock.B)
ps.mock.merged
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 1137 taxa and 5 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 5 samples by 97 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 1137 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Save the merged object
saveRDS(ps.mock.merged, file.path(path.rds, "ps.mock.merged.rds"))

11. Sequencing Quality

11.1 Load the Libraries and Paths

#Libraries:
library(dada2)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggpubr)
library(patchwork)
library(dplyr)
library(gghalves)
library(phyloseq)
library(introdataviz)
library(decontam)
library(dplyr)
library(pairwiseAdonis)
library(microbiome)
library(forcats)


#Paths:
path.rds
## [1] "Analysis/RDS"
path.figures
## [1] "Analysis/Figures"

11.2 Load the Positive Mock Samples

ps.positiveA <- readRDS (file.path(path.rds, "ps.mock.A.rds"))
ps.positiveB <- readRDS (file.path(path.rds, "ps.mockB.rds"))

11.3 Relative Abundance: Composition

Positive Mock Samples A - Plot the composition of positive control against mock community
library(phyloseq)

## Get the names of the Genus present in the samples and its abundances:

tax_Mock_A <- as.data.frame(phyloseq::tax_table(ps.positiveA))
tax_Mock_A$Genus <- as.character(tax_Mock_A$Genus)

# Replace NA and "NA" strings
tax_Mock_A$Genus[is.na(tax_Mock_A$Genus) | tax_Mock_A$Genus == "NA"] <- "Unassigned"

# Reassign cleaned tax_table
tax_table(ps.positiveA) <- phyloseq::tax_table(as.matrix(tax_Mock_A))

# Apply glomming
ps.genus.MockA <- tax_glom(ps.positiveA, taxrank = "Genus")

# Fix any NA that reappear post-glom
tax_glommed <- as.data.frame(phyloseq::tax_table(ps.genus.MockA))
tax_glommed$Genus <- as.character(tax_glommed$Genus)
tax_glommed$Genus[is.na(tax_glommed$Genus) | tax_glommed$Genus == "NA"] <- "Unassigned"
tax_table(ps.genus.MockA) <- phyloseq::tax_table(as.matrix(tax_glommed))

# Transform to relative abundance
ps.genus.rel <- transform_sample_counts(ps.genus.MockA, function(x) x / sum(x))

# Convert to dataframe for plotting or summary
df.abund <- psmelt(ps.genus.rel)

# Get the abundance table
genus_abundance <- df.abund %>%
  group_by(Genus) %>%
  summarise(total_abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(percent_abundance = (total_abundance / sum(total_abundance)) * 100) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_abundance))

genus_abundance
## # A tibble: 101 × 3
##    Genus                       total_abundance percent_abundance
##    <chr>                                 <dbl>             <dbl>
##  1 Unassigned                           0.659              22.0 
##  2 Faecalibacterium                     0.317              10.6 
##  3 Blautia                              0.310              10.3 
##  4 Subdoligranulum                      0.133               4.43
##  5 Ruminococcus                         0.123               4.11
##  6 Bacteroides                          0.114               3.79
##  7 Parabacteroides                      0.103               3.45
##  8 Fusicatenibacter                     0.0934              3.11
##  9 [Eubacterium] siraeum group          0.0811              2.70
## 10 Agathobacter                         0.0788              2.63
## # ℹ 91 more rows
# Define the top 20 genus names
top30_genera <- genus_abundance$Genus[1:30]

# Collapse less abundant genera into "Other"
df.abund.top30 <- df.abund %>%
  mutate(Genus = if_else(Genus %in% top30_genera, Genus, "Other")) %>%
  group_by(Sample, Genus) %>%
  summarise(Abundance = sum(Abundance), .groups = "drop")

# Reorder for stacking 
df.abund.top30 <- df.abund.top30 %>%
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  mutate(Genus = fct_reorder(Genus, Abundance, .desc = TRUE)) %>%
  ungroup()

# Generate random colors for each Genus
set.seed(123)  # for reproducibility
all_genera <- unique(df.abund.top30$Genus)
random_colors <- sample(colors(), length(all_genera))
genusPalette <- setNames(random_colors, all_genera)

# Plot
p.positive.MockA.2 <- ggplot(data = df.abund.top30, 
                           aes(fill = Genus, 
                               x = Sample,   
                               y = Abundance, 
                               width = 0.4)) +
  theme_bw() +   
  geom_bar(position = "stack", 
           stat = "identity", 
           alpha = 0.8) +  
  scale_fill_manual(values = genusPalette) + 
  ylab("Relative abundance") +   
  xlab("") +   
  coord_flip() +  
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),     
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),  
    legend.position = "bottom",  
    legend.direction = "horizontal",
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 7),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10)) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 10, byrow = TRUE))

print(p.positive.MockA.2)

Positive Mock Samples B - Plot the composition of positive control against mock community
## Get the names of the Genus present in the samples and its abundances:

tax_Mock_B <- as.data.frame(phyloseq::tax_table(ps.positiveB))

# Replace NA genus names with "Unassigned" 
tax_Mock_B$Genus[is.na(tax_Mock_B$Genus)] <- "Unassigned"
tax_Mock_B$Genus[tax_Mock_B$Genus == "NA"] <- "Unassigned"
tax_table(ps.positiveB) <- as.matrix(tax_Mock_B)
ps.genus.MOCKB <- tax_glom(ps.positiveB, taxrank = "Genus") # Aggregate by Genus level
 
# Transform to relative abundance
ps.genus.rel.MOCKB <- transform_sample_counts(ps.genus.MOCKB, function(x) x / sum(x))

# Convert to dataframe for plotting or summary
df.abund.MOCKB <- psmelt(ps.genus.rel.MOCKB)

genus_abundance_MockB <- df.abund.MOCKB %>%
  group_by(Genus) %>%
  summarise(total_abundance = sum(Abundance)) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_abundance))

genus_abundance_MockB
## # A tibble: 8 × 2
##   Genus                total_abundance
##   <chr>                          <dbl>
## 1 Listeria                   1.91     
## 2 Pseudomonas                0.0534   
## 3 Bacillus                   0.0225   
## 4 Unassigned                 0.0116   
## 5 Escherichia/Shigella       0.00136  
## 6 Salmonella                 0.00136  
## 7 Lactobacillus              0.0000638
## 8 Staphylococcus             0
genusPalette <- c(Bacillus="#009e73", 
                  Enterococcus="#332d2d",
                  "Escherichia/Shigella"="#0072b2", 
                  Lactobacillus="#f0e442",
                  Listeria="#ecb333", 
                  Pseudomonas="#d62728", 
                  Salmonella="#ec6e0b", 
                  Staphylococcus="#76c6f3", 
                  Unassigned="#bababa")

# Reorder Sample factor by increasing total abundance
df.abund.MOCKB <- df.abund.MOCKB %>%
  group_by(Corrected_Sample_ID) %>%
  mutate(Genus = fct_reorder(Genus, Abundance, .desc = TRUE)) %>%  # smallest to largest
  ungroup()


# Plot
p.positive.MockB <- ggplot(data = df.abund.MOCKB, 
                            aes(fill = Genus, 
                                x = Corrected_Sample_ID,   
                                y = Abundance, width = 0.4)) +
  theme_bw() + 
  geom_bar(position = "stack", 
           stat = "identity", 
           alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = genusPalette) +
  ylab("Relative abundance") +
  xlab("") +
  coord_flip() +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 10),    
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 10),  
    legend.position = "bottom",  # Bottom position
    legend.direction = "horizontal",# Horizontal layout
    legend.key.size = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.text = element_text(size = 9),
    legend.title = element_text(size = 10)) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE))  # Adjust number of rows


print(p.positive.MockB)

11.4 Sequencing depth and 16S gene copy numbers

Sample types
# Remove the mock samples from the phyloseq object
ps_wo_mock <- readRDS(file.path(path.rds, "phyloseq.rds"))
ps_wo_mock
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 24011 taxa and 164 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 164 samples by 56 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 24011 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
ps_wo_mock <- subset_samples(ps_wo_mock, Sample_Type != "Mock")
ps_wo_mock
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 24011 taxa and 157 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 157 samples by 56 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 24011 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Subsample the dataset
SAMPLE_TYPES <- c(
  "Feces",
  "Environmental_Control",
  "Extraction_Control",
  "Reagent_Control"
)

theme_set(theme_bw())

# Define color palette
ColorFillManual <- c(
  "Feces" = "#1f77b4",
  "Environmental_Control" = "#ff7f0e",
  "Extraction_Control" = "#2ca02c",
  "Reagent_Control" = "#d62728")

sdata_df <- as(sample_data(ps_wo_mock), "data.frame")
sdata_df$Sample_Type <- factor(sdata_df$Sample_Type, levels = SAMPLE_TYPES)
#sdata_df$PF_Clusters <- as.numeric(sdata_df$PF_Clusters)
# Add sequencing depth 
sdata_df$read_count <- sample_sums(ps_wo_mock)

# ---- Plot 1: Sequencing Depth ----
plot_wo_mock <- ggplot(sdata_df, aes(x = Sample_Type, y = read_count, fill = Sample_Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ColorFillManual) +
  labs(title = "Sequencing Depth by Sample Type", y = "Read Count", x = "Sample Type") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

plot_wo_mock

Mock Samples
# Remove the mock samples from the phyloseq object
ps.mock.merged <- readRDS(file.path(path.rds, "ps.mock.merged.rds"))
ps.mock.merged
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 1137 taxa and 5 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 5 samples by 97 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 1137 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Define Samples
SAMPLE_TYPES <- c(
  "Mock_A",
  "Mock_B")

# Define color palette
theme_set(theme_bw())
ColorFillManual <- c(
  "Mock_A" = "#1f77b4",
  "Mock_B" = "#ff7f0e")

sdata_df_mock <- as(sample_data(ps.mock.merged), "data.frame")
sdata_df_mock$Corrected_Sample_Type <- factor(sdata_df_mock$Corrected_Sample_Type, levels = SAMPLE_TYPES)

# Add sequencing depth 
sdata_df_mock$read_count <- sample_sums(ps.mock.merged)

# Plot 2: Sequencing Depth for Mock Samples
plot.mock.merged <- ggplot(sdata_df_mock, aes(x = Corrected_Sample_Type, y = read_count, fill = Corrected_Sample_Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ColorFillManual) +
  labs(title = "Sequencing Depth by Mock Samples", y = "Read Count", x = "Sample Type") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

plot.mock.merged

11.5 Plot No. Reads

Mock Samples
ps.mock.merged
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 1137 taxa and 5 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 5 samples by 97 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 1137 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
# Define color palette
theme_set(theme_bw())
ColorFillManual <- c(
  "Mock_A" = "#1f77b4",
  "Mock_B" = "#ff7f0e")

# plot 2. Reads by Sample Type without Mock
p.reads.mock <- ggplot(sdata_df_mock, aes(x = Corrected_Sample_Type, y = (qpcr_16s_copies_ul), fill = Corrected_Sample_Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ColorFillManual) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(title = "qPCR 16S Copy Number by Sample Type (log scale)",
       y = expression("16S copies/μL (log"[10]*")"),
       x = "Sample Type") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

print(p.reads.mock)

Samples Types
ps_wo_mock
## phyloseq-class experiment-level object
## otu_table()   OTU Table:         [ 24011 taxa and 157 samples ]
## sample_data() Sample Data:       [ 157 samples by 56 sample variables ]
## tax_table()   Taxonomy Table:    [ 24011 taxa by 7 taxonomic ranks ]
sdata_df <- as(sample_data(ps_wo_mock), "data.frame")

# Subsample the dataset
SAMPLE_TYPES <- c(
  "Feces",
  "Environmental_Control",
  "Extraction_Control",
  "Reagent_Control"
)

# Palette
theme_set(theme_bw())
ColorFillManual <- c(
  "Feces" = "#1f77b4",
  "Environmental_Control" = "#ff7f0e",
  "Extraction_Control" = "#2ca02c",
  "Reagent_Control" = "#d62728")

sdata_df$Sample_Type <- factor(sdata_df$Sample_Type, levels = SAMPLE_TYPES)
sdata_df$read_count <- sample_sums(ps_wo_mock)
sdata_df$qpcr_16s_copies_ul <- as.integer(gsub(",", "", sdata_df$qpcr_16s_copies_ul))

## plot No. Reads by Sample Type without Mock
p.reads <- ggplot(sdata_df, aes(x = Sample_Type, y = (qpcr_16s_copies_ul), fill = Sample_Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 0.6) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = ColorFillManual) +
  scale_y_log10() +
  labs(title = "qPCR 16S Copy Number by Sample Type (log scale)",
       y = expression("16S copies/μL (log"[10]*")"),
       x = "Sample Type") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

print(p.reads)
